topic: Will AI cause net job losses in the next decade?

nodes.claim: [Whether productivity shocks from technological advances follow historical patterns of job creation] AI will cause net job losses in the next decade, particularly in white-collar work, with displaced workers unable to transition to new roles and experiencing wage decline. vs. AI advancement/proliferation causes/supports unemployment rate increase vs. AI capabilities in white-collar work causes/supports job displacement in US economy vs. AI being general intelligence improving at human tasks causes/supports traditional job redeployment strategy failing vs. AI will not cause net job losses in the next decade because historical technological revolutions create productivity gains that generate new industries and labor demand, despite near-term displacement and distributional challenges. vs. technological revolutions undermines/reduces labor elimination vs. Microsoft Office advent causes/supports office worker labor demand vs. major technological advances causes/supports productivity shocks pattern vs. technological revolutions causes/supports task composition alteration

[AI capability causing economic disruption and productivity shocks] single GPU cluster replacing 10,000 white-collar workers causes/supports economic disruption vs. AI-driven automation causes/supports productivity shock

[Whether AI reduces labor demand (A says yes, B suggests AI as complement undermines this)] AI capabilities improvement undermines/reduces company demand for workers vs. AI capability in management and oversight tasks causes/supports displaced workers unable to move to AI management roles vs. AI as complement to labor undermines/reduces labor demand reduction

[Labor's economic position under high substitution between capital and labor] AI-driven displacement spiral causes/supports structural impairment of white-collar earnings power vs. extremely high elasticity of substitution between AI capital and labor causes/supports labor's share of income collapse
headcount cuts and AI tool investment causes/supports maintaining output with lower costs

[Whether new industries/roles from technological change offset job losses] AI creating new roles while rendering dozens obsolete causes/supports net negative economic outcome for workers vs. new AI-created roles paying fraction of old job wages causes/supports worker economic welfare decline vs. displaced white-collar workers taking lower-paying service/gig jobs causes/supports increased labor supply in service and gig sectors vs. increased labor supply in service and gig sectors causes/supports wage compression in those sectors vs. productivity gains causes/supports new industry generation
rising software engineer job postings (up 11% YoY) causes/supports undermines displacement narrative
marginal cost of compute rises above marginal cost of human labor undermines/reduces labor substitution by automation
persistent failure of redistribution mechanisms and simultaneous drying up of investment opportunities causes/supports structural fall in aggregate demand
physical capital constraints undermines/reduces economic deployment of automation
energy availability constraints undermines/reduces economic deployment of automation
